extraction.one_line_summary: BJERG Mikkel won by 27 seconds over Cavagna after being 20 seconds down at first split, taking yellow from Heukeleers by 12 seconds over Vingegaard.
tactical_patterns: Bjerg lost 20 seconds first 10km then demolished final 21km, suggesting late-heavy TT courses favor diesel power over explosive starts.
O'Connor 5th on stage despite GC defending role indicates UAE lacks true TT domestique depth for Grand Tour support.
Yates only 1 second down on Cavagna at checkpoint but faded badly, revealing poor 20km+ time trial endurance.

rider_signals.BJERG Mikkel: positive: First pro win after three U23 World TT titles shows he's been severely underutilized, could contest individual TT medals
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: Fifth on stage while defending GC suggests he's regained 2021 Tour form when he finished fourth overall
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: Lost 2.5 minutes and already 30 seconds down at first checkpoint shows complete time trial regression
rider_signals.GODDU David: negative: Dropped over 2 minutes despite coming off team time trial podium reveals individual/team TT skillsets don't transfer
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Strong first split but faded catastrophically suggests cannot sustain tempo beyond 15 minutes in rolling TTs
rider_signals.HEUKELEERS Jordi: neutral: Lost yellow but top-10 finish exceeded expectations, sprint legs translating to short TT competence
